questions.q: Whom did Prahasta serve as a commander and chief minister?
questions.explanation: Prahasta was the veteran general and chief minister of Ravana, the rakshasa king of Lanka.

questions.q: What kind of being was Prahasta?
questions.explanation: Prahasta was a rakshasa, one of the demon warriors of Ravana's Lanka.

questions.q: In which city did Prahasta hold his position of power?
questions.explanation: Prahasta was the commander of the armies of Lanka, Ravana's island kingdom.

questions.q: What army did Prahasta command?
questions.explanation: Prahasta was the commander of the Lankan armies of rakshasas under Ravana.

questions.q: Against which host did Prahasta fight before the walls of Lanka?
questions.explanation: Prahasta fell to the vanara host that besieged Lanka alongside Rama.

questions.q: What was the ultimate fate of Prahasta in the war?
questions.explanation: Prahasta was slain in battle before the city walls during the siege of Lanka.

questions.q: What weapon is associated with Prahasta as his emblem?
questions.explanation: Prahasta's emblem is the mace, fitting for a heavy-hitting rakshasa general.

questions.q: Which epic tells the story of Prahasta?
questions.explanation: Prahasta is a character in the Ramayana, the epic of Rama and the war against Ravana.

questions.q: Which prince led the army that opposed Prahasta at Lanka?
questions.explanation: Rama led the vanara forces in the siege of Lanka where Prahasta was killed.

questions.q: Prahasta is best described as which of the following?
questions.explanation: Prahasta was a veteran general and the chief minister of Ravana, experienced in war.

questions.q: Which vanara warrior is traditionally credited with killing Prahasta?
questions.explanation: In the Valmiki Ramayana, the vanara commander Nila slays Prahasta in battle.

questions.q: During which book (kanda) of the Ramayana does Prahasta die in battle?
questions.explanation: Prahasta is killed during the Yuddha Kanda, the war book describing the siege of Lanka.

questions.q: What court role, besides general, did Prahasta hold under Ravana?
questions.explanation: Prahasta served as Ravana's chief minister as well as the commander of his armies.

questions.q: When Ravana held council on whether to fight Rama, what did Prahasta advise?
options: To return Sita and make peace
To wage war confidently against Rama
To worship Rama as a god
questions.explanation: Prahasta boasted of the rakshasas' strength and urged Ravana to fight rather than yield Sita.

questions.q: Which rakshasa, brother of Ravana, urged peace in contrast to Prahasta's call for war?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana counseled returning Sita and making peace, opposing the war urged by Prahasta and others.

questions.q: Prahasta led his charge from which gate or quarter when sallying out against the vanaras?
options: He led a sortie out of a gate of Lanka
He stayed hidden within Ravana's palace
He attacked from a ship at sea
He never left the inner city
questions.explanation: Prahasta led a sally out from Lanka with his forces to confront the vanara army before the walls.

questions.q: What blow finally felled Prahasta in his duel with the vanara who killed him?
options: A poisoned arrow to the heart
A massive rock or boulder hurled at him
A divine weapon from the gods
questions.explanation: Nila crushed Prahasta by hurling a great rock that smashed his head during their combat.

questions.q: How did Ravana react to the news of Prahasta's death?
options: He was grieved and enraged, resolving to fight himself
questions.explanation: Ravana was stricken with grief and fury at losing his trusted general Prahasta and prepared to take the field.

questions.q: Prahasta was renowned among the rakshasas chiefly for which quality?
options: His prowess and experience in warfare
questions.explanation: Prahasta was celebrated as a mighty, veteran warrior and the foremost of Ravana's generals.

questions.q: Whose abduction set in motion the war in which Prahasta fought and died?
options: The abduction of Sita by Ravana
questions.explanation: Ravana's abduction of Sita led to Rama's siege of Lanka, the war in which Prahasta perished.

questions.q: Before the great war, Prahasta is associated with which earlier campaign of Ravana's rise to power?
options: Ravana's conquests against gods and kings
The building of the bridge to Lanka
The exile of Rama to the forest
questions.explanation: As a longtime general, Prahasta is linked in tradition to Ravana's earlier conquests that built his empire.

questions.q: In several accounts, to whom was Prahasta related as a kinsman of Ravana's line?
options: He was an uncle/kinsman of Ravana through the rakshasa lineage
questions.explanation: Tradition places Prahasta as a senior kinsman within Ravana's rakshasa lineage, often described as a relative such as an uncle.

questions.q: Which vanara chief who slew Prahasta was himself a notable commander of Rama's army?
options: Nila, a leader of the vanara forces
Dadhimukha, keeper of the honey grove
questions.explanation: Nila, who killed Prahasta, was one of the principal vanara generals in Rama's host.

questions.q: Prahasta's charioteer is killed before him in the fight, leaving Prahasta to do what?
options: Fight on the ground or from a broken chariot
questions.explanation: With his chariot disabled, Prahasta continued fighting on foot or from the wreck before Nila slew him.

questions.q: Which of these rakshasa generals fell BEFORE Prahasta in the sequence of Lanka's losses in the Yuddha Kanda?
options: Lesser rakshasa captains in early skirmishes
questions.explanation: Prahasta was among the first great commanders to fall; minor captains died in earlier skirmishes, while Kumbhakarna, Indrajit, and Ravana fell later.

questions.q: What did Prahasta's death signify for the morale of the rakshasa army?
options: A serious blow that demoralized the defenders
questions.explanation: Losing their veteran commander Prahasta dismayed the rakshasas and emboldened the vanaras.

questions.q: After Prahasta's fall, who personally entered the battlefield, intensifying the war?
questions.explanation: Enraged by Prahasta's death, Ravana himself came out to fight, escalating the conflict.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, Prahasta is described leading his troops with what kind of standard or banner motif?
options: A fearsome war standard befitting Ravana's chief general
questions.explanation: Prahasta, as Ravana's foremost general, advanced under a martial standard fitting a great rakshasa commander.

questions.q: Which statement best captures Prahasta's narrative role in the Ramayana?
options: A loyal veteran commander whose death marks an early turning point in the siege
A repentant rakshasa who joins Rama
A trickster who deceives both sides
A minor servant with no battlefield role
questions.explanation: Prahasta is a loyal, formidable general whose death is one of the early major losses signaling Lanka's coming defeat.

questions.q: Among Ravana's high command, where does Prahasta rank in seniority and authority?
options: He was the chief among Ravana's generals and ministers
He was the lowest-ranking foot soldier
He was a neutral envoy with no command
questions.explanation: Prahasta was the foremost of Ravana's generals and his chief minister, second in authority on military matters.
